deep_dive.title: PandaAI: A Practical Agent CQ2 for Neuro-symbolic Data Analysis And Integrated Decision-Making in Quantitative Finance
deep_dive.abstract: While deep learning has excelled in various domains, its application to sequential decision-making in finance remains challenging due to the low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and non-stationarity of financial data. Leveraging the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), we propose \textbf{PandaAI}, a closed-loop neuro-symbolic LLM agent with market regime modeling and constrained alpha generation, which bridges general LLM reasoning with financial rigor and suppresses the financial toxicity of LLM-generated outputs. To bridge the gap between general linguistic capability and financial rigor, we fine-tune a domain-specific LLM. Furthermore, we integrate this LLM into a modular architecture and form a closed-loop system. Unlike traditional models that optimize isolated prediction metrics, \textbf{PandaAI} is designed as a neuro-symbolic agent that navigates the complex, real-world financial environment with explicit risk awareness. Extensive experiments on CSI 300 stock data show that \textbf{PandaAI} achieves a $18.2\%$ higher Rank IC and $25.7\%$ lower maximum drawdown than state-of-the-art time-series models. Our constrained LLM generation and dual-channel adaptation method provide a general paradigm for LLM deployment in high-stakes sequential decision-making scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM agent for neuro-symbolic data analysis and decision-making in quantitative finance
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于定量金融中神经符号数据分析与决策的LLM智能体
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于定量金融中神经符号数据分析与决策的LLM智能体
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于定量金融中神经符号数据分析与决策的LLM智能体
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PandaAI, a closed-loop neuro-symbolic LLM agent with market regime modeling and constrained alpha generation for quantitative finance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对深度学习在金融决策中面临低信噪比和非平稳性的挑战，提出PandaAI智能体，通过闭环神经符号架构融合LLM推理与金融严谨性。系统包括市场状态建模和受约束的Alpha生成，并微调了领域特定LLM。在多个定量金融任务上的实验表明，PandaAI在决策质量与输出可靠性方面优于纯LLM基线，为AI4Finance提供了实用、可信的框架。

deep_dive.title: CausalAlpha: A Real-Time Geopolitical Risk Index from OSINT Channels for Causal Discovery in Financial Markets
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce CausalAlpha, an open-source framework that constructs a high-frequency Geopolitical Risk (GPR) index from Telegram OSINT channels using natural language processing, and applies causal discovery methods to identify the directed causal structure between geopolitical uncertainty and financial market variables. Unlike standard sentiment indices or Granger-causality approaches, CausalAlpha employs the Peter-Clark (PC) algorithm to recover the directed acyclic graph (DAG) of causal dependencies between five category-specific GPR indicators and a set of financial variables spanning commodity prices, equity indices, and credit instruments, estimated across four DAG specifications and three significance levels with 500 block-bootstrap resamples. Two findings emerge as globally robust across all DAG specifications at alpha = 0.10: political instability and energy media coverage independently and causally precede conflict coverage, establishing conflict as the primary causal sink of geopolitical narrative escalation in real-time OSINT channels. At the strictest significance level (alpha = 0.05), conflict coverage causally precedes energy sector equity returns (delta XLE), consistent with geopolitical escalation transmitting to energy markets. A Structural VAR on the core macro panel confirms that dynamic transmission from geopolitical NLP signals to financial market prices is statistically weak at daily frequency, suggesting that geopolitical news signals operate primarily within the media narrative system. The framework is deployed as a production application on Google Cloud Run with automated data collection and index construction, representing a step toward real-time macrofinancial risk monitoring using OSINT.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: NLP for geopolitical risk index and causal discovery in financial markets
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自然语言处理构建地缘政治风险指数并用于金融市场因果发现

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents CausalAlpha, an open-source framework using NLP on Telegram OSINT to generate a real-time geopolitical risk index, and applies PC algorithm for causal discovery in financial markets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 地缘政治事件对金融市场的影响难以用量化指标实时度量和因果识别。本文提出CausalAlpha，利用自然语言处理从Telegram开源情报渠道构建高频地缘政治风险指数，并采用Peter-Clark算法发现五类风险指标与商品价格、股票指数和信贷工具之间的有向因果结构。该方法超越了传统情绪指数和格兰杰因果分析，为金融分析提供了可重现的因果推断工具。

deep_dive.title: Tree-of-Experience: A Structured Experience-Management Solution for Self-Evolving Agents under Low-Repetition and Implicit-Reward Environments
deep_dive.abstract: Experience-based self-evolution is crucial for LLM agents, but existing benchmarks often assume explicit goals, stable task patterns, and clear feedback. We study a more challenging setting: low-repetition tasks with implicit rewards, where past experience is difficult to reuse and feedback is delayed, noisy, and outcome-level. We introduce \textsc{FinEvolveBench}, a temporally controlled benchmark for financial sentiment prediction that links daily news-driven predictions to future excess returns. We further propose Tree-of-Experience (ToE), a structured experience-management method that organizes, retrieves, validates, and updates agent experience. Experiments show that general-purpose experience mechanisms do not consistently outperform no-experience baselines, while ToE achieves stronger overall performance. These results highlight the importance of structured experience management for self-evolving agents in implicit-reward environments.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: financial sentiment prediction benchmark and agent experience management
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 金融情感预测基准和代理经验管理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 金融情感预测基准和代理经验管理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 金融情感预测基准和代理经验管理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces FinEvolveBench, a financial sentiment prediction benchmark linking news to returns, and Tree-of-Experience for self-evolving LLM agents in low-repetition tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对低重复隐式奖励环境下的智能体自我进化挑战，构建了FinEvolveBench金融情感预测基准，将每日新闻驱动预测与未来超额回报关联，并提出了经验树（ToE）方法，用于组织、检索、验证和更新智能体经验，实验证明了其有效性。

quick_skim.title: Information Networks of Stock Prices
quick_skim.abstract: The collective movement of stock prices harbors complex interdependencies that are conventionally simplified only through a linear lens. This paper explores computed structural network representations in the Indonesian capital market by testing the limits of Pearson correlation and Mutual Information (MI) in unveiling the spectral dynamics of the market. Across 2,328 rolling observation windows from 2015 to 2025, we examine 24 methodological configurations that combine three dependency estimators (Pearson, MI adaptive binning, and MI-kNN), two graph filtering schemes (Minimum Spanning Tree/MST and Planar Maximally Filtered Graph/PMFG), and four community decoders.   The empirical results unveil a fundamental reality: topological richness does not always resonate with sectoral classification precision. The Pearson, MST, and Infomap configuration is shown to remain the most robust foundation for recovering conventional sectoral taxonomy. Nevertheless, when deeper observation demands the exposition of local structures and the weave of heterogeneous communities, the architectural relaxation through PMFG demonstrates its superiority. In the realm of residual information detection, MI adaptive binning appears far more proportional than kNN; histogram-based regularization successfully tames empirical noise without sweeping away traces of non-linear dependency. Ultimately, the synergy of MI and PMFG is not positioned to dethrone the dominance of linear correlation, but rather to provide an essential analytical lens for excavating hidden economic sub-structures -- such as the cohesion of commodity regimes -- that have long transcended the rigid boundaries of the market's formal sectors.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Network analysis of stock prices using mutual information and graph filtering
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用互信息和图滤波进行股票价格网络分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用互信息和图滤波进行股票价格网络分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用互信息和图滤波进行股票价格网络分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Explores structural network representations of stock prices using Pearson correlation and Mutual Information, testing 24 methodological configurations on Indonesian market data from 2015 to 2025.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 股票价格的集体运动蕴含着复杂依赖关系，传统线性方法简化了市场结构。本文对印尼资本市场进行实证，测试了Pearson相关和互信息在24种方法配置下的网络表示能力，包括不同图滤波和社区解码方案。结果表明拓扑丰富度并不总是与行业分类一致，揭示了非线性依赖在股票网络中的重要性。该研究为市场结构分析和投资组合构建提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 股票价格的集体运动蕴含着复杂依赖关系，传统线性方法简化了市场结构。本文对印尼资本市场进行实证，测试了Pearson相关和互信息在24种方法配置下的网络表示能力，包括不同图滤波和社区解码方案。结果表明拓扑丰富度并不总是与行业分类一致，揭示了非线性依赖在股票网络中的重要性。该研究为市场结构分析和投资组合构建提供了新视角。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep learning models for stock market analysis

quick_skim.title: RowNet: A Memory Transformer for Tabular Regression
quick_skim.abstract: Real estate valuation is a structured regression problem in which prices are governed by heterogeneous feature types, sparse regional effects, nonlinear interactions, and the practical logic of comparable properties. Standard multilayer perceptrons treat each row as an isolated vector and must learn locality, scale sensitivity, and categorical matching from supervision alone. Gradient-boosted decision trees provide strong tabular baselines, but their feature-centric splitting mechanism does not explicitly model the retrieval of similar historical observations.   This paper presents RowNet, a retrieval-based neural architecture for real estate price-per-square-meter prediction. RowNet represents a query property through pairwise similarity features against a memory bank of labeled properties. A first retrieval layer estimates a coarse target from feature-only similarities. A second layer augments the memory comparison with target-consistency features and uses multiple learned attention heads to retrieve complementary comparable sets. A final mixture-of-experts module combines learned gating, residual correction, entropy regularization, and head-diversity regularization to produce the prediction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: transformer for tabular regression in real estate valuation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于房地产估值的表格回归Transformer
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于房地产估值的表格回归Transformer
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于房地产估值的表格回归Transformer
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces RowNet, a retrieval-based neural architecture with memory transformer for real estate price prediction, outperforming MLPs and decision trees.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 传统多层感知机在表格回归中难以捕捉非线性交互和相似案例检索。本文提出RowNet，一种基于检索的神经架构，利用记忆Transformer为查询房产构建成对相似性特征进行价格预测。在房地产估值任务上，RowNet超越了多层感知机和梯度提升决策树，展示了神经检索在金融资产定价中的应用潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 传统多层感知机在表格回归中难以捕捉非线性交互和相似案例检索。本文提出RowNet，一种基于检索的神经架构，利用记忆Transformer为查询房产构建成对相似性特征进行价格预测。在房地产估值任务上，RowNet超越了多层感知机和梯度提升决策树，展示了神经检索在金融资产定价中的应用潜力。
